Theoretical calculation method of BVG combustion and explosion characteristics. (a) A is the flame front area, Q ˙ is the volume flow of unburned gas, f(x) is the flame profile function, and S L is the LFS [113,114] (Adapted with permission [113], Copyright 2022, Elsevier), (b) S b is the flame propagation speed, dr/dt is the flame radius change rate, k is the stretching rate, L b is the Markstein length, and C is the flame curvature [115,116]. (Adapted with permission [104], Copyright 2024, Elsevier), (c) S u 0 is the LBV, ρ b is density with respect to the burnt state, ρ u is density with respect to the unburnt state [117], (d) V is the volume of the explosion-proof sphere, (dp/dt)max is the maximum pressure rate [118], (e) L mix is the FL or EL of the BVG, L i represents the FL or EL of a certain component in the BVG, and x i represents the volume percentage of a certain component in the BVG, (f) L m ix ′ is the FL or EL considering the effect of inert gas, B is the volume fraction of CO2 in BVG,(g) T is the initial temperature, P is the initial pressure.
